_doc: Unified schema-process artifact combining all 15 planning JSON contracts and LLM-call specs for the prefetch pipeline.

process_sequence.purpose: Runtime product context to NeedSet output, planning context, and Schema 4 planner contract.
Brand/domain sidecar resolution used to enrich search planning and host promotion.
Schema 3 planning context to Schema 4 planner output and search-plan handoff.
Schema 4 handoff through adapter, guard, execution context, classification, and rerank/triage contracts.
Final provider-facing query payload entering the search execution layer.

content._doc: Schema 1 (NeedSetStartInput) field-by-field data source mapping. Updated 2026-03-17 against current seed, discovery, bootstrap, and finalization call sites.
content.schema_version: needset_start_input.v2 — input contract only. computeNeedSet() emits 'needset_output.v2'; Schema 1 itself is not emitted as a runtime artifact.

run.run_id: SOURCE: seedRound0NeedSet() passes ''; bootstrapRunProductExecutionState.js, runDiscoverySeedPlan.js, and buildNeedsetReasoningContext.js pass the active runId.
run.category: SOURCE: job.category — passed at all 4 call sites
run.product_id: SOURCE: job.productId || s3Key — passed at all 4 call sites
run.brand: SOURCE: job.identityLock?.brand || '' — passed from all main call sites
run.model: SOURCE: job.identityLock?.model || '' — passed from all main call sites
run.base_model: RESOLVED: Passed by bootstrapRunProductExecutionState.js, runDiscoverySeedPlan.js, and buildNeedsetReasoningContext.js via identityLock/base model context.
run.aliases: RESOLVED: Passed by bootstrapRunProductExecutionState.js, runDiscoverySeedPlan.js, and buildNeedsetReasoningContext.js via job aliases or identityLock aliases.
run.round: SOURCE: seedRound0NeedSet() and bootstrapRunProductExecutionState.js pass 0; runDiscoverySeedPlan.js passes roundContext.round || 0; buildNeedsetReasoningContext.js omits it so computeNeedSet() defaults to 0.
run.round_mode: SOURCE: seedRound0NeedSet() and bootstrapRunProductExecutionState.js pass 'seed'; runDiscoverySeedPlan.js passes roundContext.round_mode || 'seed'; buildNeedsetReasoningContext.js omits it so computeNeedSet() defaults to 'seed'.

identity.state: SOURCE: needsetEngine.js:526 via mapIdentityState(identityContext). Maps 'locked'|'provisional'|'conflict' → PanelIdentityState.
identity.source_label_state: SOURCE: needsetEngine.js:527 via deriveSourceLabelState(identityContext). Uses status + confidence + contradiction_count.
identity.publishable: DROPPED: Not relevant for indexing/planning phase. Review/publish decisions happen downstream.
identity.review_required: DROPPED: Not relevant for indexing/planning phase. Review phase handles this.

settings._note: DROPPED FROM SCHEMA 1: Settings flow directly to Phase 2 via searchPlanningContext.js:derivePlannerLimits(). The settings param in computeNeedSet() is declared but never read. Config reaches the planner without Schema 1 as intermediary.

fields._note: Array of per-field objects. Below documents the source for each sub-field.
fields.field_key: SOURCE: needsetEngine.js:342 — collectFieldKeys() unions fieldOrder + provenance keys + fieldRules keys.
fields.label: SOURCE: needsetEngine.js:418 — String(rule.display_name || rule.ui?.label || field).
fields.group_key: SOURCE: needsetEngine.js:419 — String(rule.group || ''). From field_rules runtime JSON.

history._note: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories.js builds per-field history after each round. Discovery passes roundContext.previousFieldHistories into computeNeedSet(), and finalization can merge the current round through enrichNeedSetFieldHistories.js.
history.existing_queries: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories merges searchPlanQueries[].target_fields → per-field query lists. Deduplicates via Set union.
history.domains_tried: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories unions prev.domains_tried + evidence[].rootDomain from current round provenance.
history.host_classes_tried: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories classifies each evidence entry via classifyHostClass(tier, tierName, host) → HostClass enum. Unions with previous.
history.evidence_classes_tried: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories classifies each evidence entry via classifyEvidenceClass(tier, tierName, method, host, url) → EvidenceClass enum. Unions with previous.
history.query_count: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories increments by count of new queries targeting this field per round.
history.urls_examined_count: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories counts unique evidence URLs per field per round and accumulates.
history.no_value_attempts: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories increments when field was targeted by queries but field value remains unknown.
history.duplicate_attempts_suppressed: RESOLVED: buildFieldHistories distributes round-level suppression count to all targeted fields.

details.status: RESOLVED — present at bootstrap, discovery seed, and finalization call sites
RESOLVED — present at bootstrap, discovery seed, and finalization call sites
DROPPED — config flows directly to Phase 2, not through Schema 1
DROPPED — informational only, actual value lives in config
DROPPED — informational only, actual value is hardcoded
RESOLVED — buildFieldHistories() feeds discovery/finalization round history

content._doc: Schema 2 (NeedSetOutput) gap analysis. Compares spec (Data - Flow - Schemas.md) against actual computeNeedSet() output (needsetEngine.js). Updated 2026-03-17.

search_exhausted.actual: needsetEngine.js — derived from field history: no_value_attempts >= 3 AND evidence_classes_tried >= 3

fields._shape_note: Array of per-field objects. Individual field shape analysis below.

reasons.status: MATCH — note: tier_pref_unmet reserved for future (line 257)

step_1_normalize_field_rows.lowercase_trim_query_terms: RESOLVED — normalizeStringArray() in needsetEngine.js lowercases, trims, and deduplicates.
step_1_normalize_field_rows.normalize_domain_hints_canonical_host: RESOLVED — normalizeDomainHints() in needsetEngine.js strips protocol/path, lowercases, deduplicates.
step_1_normalize_field_rows.deduplicate_preferred_content_types: RESOLVED — normalizeStringArray() in needsetEngine.js deduplicates via Set.
step_1_normalize_field_rows.deduplicate_existing_queries: RESOLVED — buildFieldHistories uses Set union (deduped). needsetEngine.js:294 carries forward from previousFieldHistories which is already deduped.

_logic_box_1_compliance.step_2_no_top_level_hints: MATCH — hints stay per-field in idx block.
_logic_box_1_compliance.step_3_score_each_field: MATCH — need_score, summary, blockers, unresolved/critical lists all computed.
_logic_box_1_compliance.step_4_preserve_field_history: MATCH — deriveFieldHistory() carries forward, now fed by buildFieldHistories.
_logic_box_1_compliance.step_5_emit_field_first_artifact: MATCH — fields array is primary output.

_ghost_consumers._investigation_result: CONFIRMED DEAD CODE. All 7 ghost properties are never set anywhere in the codebase. Consumers use defensive fallbacks (|| [], || null, toArray()) that silently produce empty values. Test mocks create these properties manually, hiding the mismatch.

needs.accessed_shape: Array of { field_key, need_score, required_level, min_refs }
needs.status: DEAD CODE — never set, always resolves to []. Equivalent data lives in computeNeedSet().fields[] (Schema 2).
needs.cleanup: Remove reads from 4 consumers. If needed, migrate to use Schema 2 fields[] array which has need_score, required_level, min_refs.

needset_size.status: DEAD CODE — always undefined. Equivalent: summary.total - summary.resolved.
needset_size.cleanup: Replace with rows.length or summary derivation.

identity_lock_state.status: DEAD CODE — always null. Equivalent data: identity.state from Schema 2 output.

identity_audit_rows.status: DEAD CODE — always undefined/falsy. No equivalent in computeNeedSet output.
identity_audit_rows.cleanup: Remove reads or source from identityContext if needed.

reason_counts.status: DEAD CODE — always undefined. Derivable from fields[].reasons aggregation if needed.
reason_counts.cleanup: Remove reads or derive from Schema 2 fields[].

required_level_counts.status: DEAD CODE — always undefined. Derivable from fields[].required_level aggregation.
required_level_counts.cleanup: Remove reads or derive from Schema 2 fields[].

snapshots.status: DEAD CODE — always resolves to []. No equivalent.

gaps.status: RESOLVED — normalizeStringArray() and normalizeDomainHints() added to extractSearchHints() in needsetEngine.js. 7 new tests pass.
RESOLVED — now derived from field history: no_value_attempts >= 3 AND evidence_classes_tried.length >= 3. 3 new tests pass.
OPEN — cosmetic. Optional field. Can fix when convenient.
INVESTIGATED — CONFIRMED DEAD CODE. Property never set anywhere. 4 consumers read undefined → fallback to []. Tests mock it manually. Safe to remove reads or migrate to Schema 2 fields[].

gaps.field: Ghost properties: needset_size, identity_lock_state, identity_audit_rows, reason_counts, required_level_counts, snapshots
gaps.status: INVESTIGATED — CONFIRMED DEAD CODE. All 6 properties never set anywhere. Consumers use defensive fallbacks. Safe to remove or migrate.

gaps.description: run_id, category, product_id, generated_at, total_fields, focus_fields, bundles, profile_mix, and rows are not in the formal Schema 2 spec but are still emitted and consumed by needsetStoryProjection and the runtime bridge. These are legacy bridge keys.
gaps.fix: NOT URGENT. These can coexist with Schema 2 keys. Migration path: consumers should migrate to using Schema 2 fields (for example, 'fields' instead of 'rows', 'summary' instead of 'total_fields'). Remove backward-compat keys once all consumers are migrated.

content._doc: Schema 3 (SearchPlanningContext) gap analysis. Compares spec against actual buildSearchPlanningContext() output (searchPlanningContext.js:288-377) and downstream consumer buildSearchPlan() (searchPlanBuilder.js:263-417). Updated 2026-03-16 — all body text aligned with code after gap fixes.

_plan_alignment._question: Are we following the plans to achieve 00-PRODUCT-GOAL.md?
_plan_alignment.answer: YES. The pipeline brain is now connected to its memory. Schema 3 computes anti-garbage intelligence (domains_tried, host_classes_tried, evidence_classes_tried, no_value_attempts, search_exhaustion) and inlines catalog metadata (source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class). Schema 4 buildSearchPlan sends ALL of these to the LLM in the payload (searchPlanBuilder.js:298-338). The feedback loop from buildFieldHistories → Schema 2 → Schema 3 → Schema 4 LLM payload is complete.

current_status.anti_garbage_signals: CONNECTED — searchPlanBuilder.js:313-335 sends domains_tried_union, host_classes_tried_union, evidence_classes_tried_union, no_value_attempts, preferred_content_types_union, aliases_union, weak_field_keys, conflict_field_keys, host_class, source_target, search_intent, urls_examined_count, query_count to the LLM
current_status.catalog_metadata: INLINED — searchPlanningContext.js:249-259 inlines label, desc, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class from catalog into each focus_group
current_status.search_exhaustion: ACTIVE — searchPlanningContext.js:143-149 + 204-206 + 232-237 compute exhaustion and move fully-exhausted groups to hold phase
current_status.aliases: FLOWING — searchPlanningContext.js:175,214,282 aggregates aliases_union per group; searchPlanBuilder.js:325 sends to LLM
current_status.feedback_loop: COMPLETE — buildFieldHistories → previousFieldHistories → computeNeedSet → field.history → buildSearchPlanningContext → focus_groups unions → buildSearchPlan LLM payload

_spec_vs_actual_summary.top_level_key_match: MATCH — all spec keys present, plus field_priority_map (used by Schema 4 for bundle fields[] derivation)

needset._note: Slim projection of Schema 2 output. Built in searchPlanningContext.js:347-354. Heavy fields[] passthrough removed to reduce payload size.

missing_critical_fields.status: MATCH — consumed by searchPlanBuilder.js:306 (sent to LLM)

unresolved_fields.status: MATCH — not consumed by planner (per-group unresolved_field_keys used instead)

existing_queries.status: MATCH — consumed by searchPlanBuilder.js:337,381 (sent to LLM + used for dedup)

planner_limits._note: Derived from runtime config in searchPlanningContext.js:57-76 (derivePlannerLimits function).

phase2LlmEnabled.status: MATCH — guards LLM call in searchPlanBuilder.js:277

discoveryMaxQueries.status: MATCH — sent to LLM at searchPlanBuilder.js:308

maxUrlsPerProduct.status: MATCH — sent to LLM at searchPlanBuilder.js:309

group_catalog._note: Static metadata map built from GROUP_DEFAULTS + fieldGroupsData. Still emitted as separate top-level key but now ALSO inlined into focus_groups. Catalog is redundant but harmless.
group_catalog._source: searchPlanningContext.js:108-134 (buildGroupCatalog). GROUP_DEFAULTS has 11 mouse-specific groups. resolveGroupMeta checks fieldGroupsData first for category-aware overrides.

focus_groups._note: Derived from Schema 2 fields[] grouped by group_key. Built in searchPlanningContext.js:151-285 (buildFocusGroup). Catalog metadata inlined. Anti-garbage unions from non-accepted fields only. Sorted by phase→priority→key.

label.actual: MATCH — searchPlanningContext.js:254 — cat.label || groupKey (inlined from catalog)

search_exhausted_field_keys.status: MATCH — drives phase=hold when all non-accepted fields are exhausted

exact_match_count.consumed_by: searchPlanBuilder.js not directly — used for Schema 4 panel

query_terms_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:275 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

domain_hints_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:276 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only
domain_hints_union.consumed_by: searchPlanBuilder.js:289,323 — dead domains filtered then sent to LLM (capped to 5)

preferred_content_types_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:277 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

existing_queries_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:278 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only
existing_queries_union.consumed_by: searchPlanBuilder.js:290-293,324 — dead hashes filtered then sent to LLM (capped to 5)

domains_tried_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:279 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

host_classes_tried_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:280 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

evidence_classes_tried_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:281 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

aliases_union.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:282 — unionSorted() from non-accepted fields only

phase.actual: searchPlanningContext.js:236-247 — derived from hasUnresolved + priority + hasCoreGroupAnywhere + allFieldsExhausted
phase.consumed_by: searchPlanBuilder.js:286 — filters groups to now|next for LLM

field_priority_map._note: Map of field_key → required_level. Built in searchPlanningContext.js:332-335. Used by Schema 4 assembleSchema4() to derive bundle.fields[].bucket.

learning._note: Optional passthrough from external learning stores. searchPlanningContext.js:375 — learning || null.

previous_round_fields._note: Optional passthrough for round-over-round delta computation. searchPlanningContext.js:376.

_gap_summary._history: All 12 gaps (PROFILE-GAP-1 through PROFILE-GAP-12) resolved. Catalog metadata inlined into focus_groups. Anti-garbage signals sent to LLM. aliases_union aggregated. Search exhaustion drives phase=hold. base_model/aliases in run block. Union sets filtered to non-accepted fields. resolveGroupMeta checks fieldGroupsData. urls_examined_count/query_count aggregated. previous_round_fields consumed by computeDeltas. key standardized. llmPlanApiKey removed from spec. weak/conflict/missing_critical sent to LLM.

content._doc: Schema 4 (NeedSetPlannerOutput) gap analysis. Compares spec (Data-Flow-Schemas.md lines 420-574) against actual assembleSchema4() output (searchPlanBuilder.js:130-261). Traces every spec field to its data source [S3|LLM|POST] and maps to current implementation status. Updated 2026-03-17.

_plan_alignment._question: Does Schema 4 fulfill its two jobs? (1) Feed the current runtime-ops Schema 4 panels, (2) hand off deterministic search queries into discovery execution.
_plan_alignment.answer: YES — Schema 4 fulfills both panel and handoff jobs. assembleSchema4() (searchPlanBuilder.js:130-261) emits full panel payload with bundles, profile_influence (14 keys), deltas, round/round_mode, and per-bundle fields[]/query_family_mix/reason_active. Handoff delivers deduplicated queries with hashes into discovery execution. Learning writeback uses canonical key names.

schema_4_two_jobs.job_1_panel: COMPLETE. assembleSchema4 emits panel.identity, panel.summary, panel.blockers, panel.round, panel.round_mode, panel.bundles (with key, label, desc, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class, query_family_mix, reason_active, queries[{q,family}], fields[{key,state,bucket}]), panel.profile_influence (14 keys), and panel.deltas for the runtime-ops Schema 4 views.
schema_4_two_jobs.job_2_handoff: COMPLETE. search_plan_handoff contains queries with q/family/query_hash/group_key/target_fields/preferred_domains/exact_match_required. Hashes are deduplicated and handed to discovery execution.
schema_4_two_jobs.job_3_learning: COMPLETE. learning_writeback uses canonical key names: query_hashes_generated, queries_generated, families_used, domains_targeted, groups_activated, duplicates_suppressed.

_plan_alignment.what_must_change: Nothing — all gaps resolved as of searchPlanBuilder.js refactor.

_spec_vs_actual_summary.structural_diff: None — actual matches spec section names.

duplicates_suppressed.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:222 emits dupesDropped counter tracked during post-LLM dedup loop

targeted_exceptions.spec: number [LLM] — exceptional queries added outside normal rules

panel._summary: The panel block is the primary consumer contract for the current runtime-ops Schema 4 surfaces (`PrefetchNeedSetPanel`, `PrefetchSearchProfilePanel`, and related bundle/delta views). All sub-objects and scalar fields are emitted.

identity.spec: object [S3] — { state, manufacturer, model, confidence, official_domain, support_domain }

summary.spec: object [S3] — { total, resolved, core_total, core_unresolved, secondary_total, secondary_unresolved, optional_total, optional_unresolved, conflicts }

blockers.spec: object [S3] — { missing, weak, conflict, needs_exact_match, search_exhausted }

_spec_shape.manufacturer_html: number [POST] — count of queries with this family

profile_influence.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:241-249 emits full 14-key object: 7 family counts (initialized to 0, counted from queries), plus duplicates_suppressed, focused_bundles, targeted_exceptions, total_queries, trusted_host_share, docs_manual_share

bundles._spec_shape: Array of { key, label, desc, priority, phase, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class, query_family_mix, reason_active, queries[], fields[] }

key.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:149 emits key: gk (from fg.key || fg.group_key)

query_family_mix.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:186 attaches lg?.query_family_mix || null from LLM response groups matched by key

reason_active.spec: string|null [LLM] — why this group is being searched
reason_active.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:187 attaches lg?.reason_active || null from LLM response groups matched by key

queries.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:188 projects bundle.queries to { q: q.q, family: q.family }. Full query objects kept in search_plan_handoff.queries.

fields.spec: Array<{ key, state, bucket }> [POST] — derived from Schema 3 field breakdowns + field_priority_map
fields.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:194-198 derives fields[] from working data: state from satisfied/weak/conflict/missing sets, bucket from requiredLevelToBucket(fpm[fk])

deltas.spec: Array<{ field, from, to }> [POST] — compare previous_round_fields vs current field states
deltas.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:250 calls computeDeltas(ctx) (lines 93-115) which iterates previous_round_fields, compares to current state from focus_groups, emits { field, from, to } for changes. Round 0 returns [].

search_plan_handoff._note: Spec and actual both use 'search_plan_handoff' — naming aligned.

queries.spec: Array<{ q, query_hash, family, group_key, target_fields, preferred_domains, exact_match_required }> [POST]
queries.actual: MATCH — searchPlanBuilder.js:227 emits full query objects

query_hashes_generated.spec: string[] — hashes of all generated queries

queries_generated.spec: string[] — raw query text of all generated queries

_gap_summary._history: 16 gaps (GAP-1 through GAP-12f) identified on 2026-03-15 were all resolved in the searchPlanBuilder.js refactor. assembleSchema4() now reads group_catalog/focus_groups for bundle display fields, derives fields[] per bundle, computes full profile_influence, computes deltas, passes through LLM planner metadata, and uses canonical learning_writeback key names.

content._doc: Brand Resolver input contract for phase 2. Documents the real inputs feeding resolveBrandDomain() and the gating around its call site in searchDiscovery(). Updated 2026-03-16.
content.schema_version: brand_resolver_input.runtime.v1 - boundary is resolveBrandDomain({ brand, category, config, callLlmFn, storage }) in brandResolver.js:1-52.

call_boundary.brand: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:181-187 passes variables.brand, which comes from the phase identity lock. searchDiscovery.js:169-171 skips the resolver when brand is empty.
call_boundary.category: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:181-187 passes variables.category, derived from job.category || categoryConfig.category.
call_boundary.config: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:181-187 passes config through unchanged. resolveBrandDomain() itself only forwards config into callLlmFn; the main LLM gating happens before the call.
call_boundary.callLlmFn: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:173-178 constructs createBrandResolverCallLlm(...) only when config.llmEnabled && hasLlmRouteApiKey(config, { role: 'triage' }). Otherwise null is passed.
call_boundary.storage: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:181-187 passes storage through unchanged for cache read/write via getBrandDomain() and upsertBrandDomain().

runtime_gates.missing_brand: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:169-171 sets skip_reason = 'no_brand_in_identity_lock' and does not call resolveBrandDomain() when variables.brand is empty.
runtime_gates.triage_api_key: SOURCE: searchDiscovery.js:173-178 requires hasLlmRouteApiKey(config, { role: 'triage' }) before constructing callLlmFn.
runtime_gates.cache_short_circuit: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:14-25 returns cached official_domain/support_domain/aliases immediately when storage.getBrandDomain(brandKey, categoryKey) yields a row.
runtime_gates.llm_fallback: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:27 returns the empty output object when callLlmFn is null.
runtime_gates.error_fallback: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:51-54 returns the empty output object on any resolver exception.

storage_contract.getBrandDomain: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:14-25 expects storage.getBrandDomain(brandKey, categoryKey) to return cached official_domain, aliases, support_domain, and optionally confidence.
storage_contract.cached_aliases_shape: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:17 and parseAliases() at 56-64 accepts either a JSON string or an array for cached aliases.
storage_contract.upsertBrandDomain: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:40-49 writes brand, category, official_domain, aliases as a JSON string, support_domain, and confidence = 0.8 after an LLM resolution.

llm_adapter_contract.role: SOURCE: createBrandResolverCallLlm() in discoveryLlmAdapters.js:65-86 uses role = 'triage' and reason = 'brand_resolution'.
llm_adapter_contract.system_prompt: SOURCE: discoveryLlmAdapters.js:72-77 asks for the official domain, aliases, support subdomain, and a short reasoning array.
llm_adapter_contract.json_schema: SOURCE: brandResolverSchema() in discoveryLlmAdapters.js:1-13 requires official_domain and allows aliases, support_domain, and reasoning.

normalization.brand_trim: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:7 trims brand into brandKey.
normalization.category_trim: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:8 trims category into categoryKey.
normalization.official_domain: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:36 lowercases and trims the LLM result.
normalization.aliases: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:37 lowercases, trims, and filters empty alias strings.
normalization.support_domain: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:38 lowercases and trims the LLM result.
normalization.reasoning: SOURCE: brandResolver.js:39 trims reasoning strings and filters empty entries.

details.status: CLOSED — host_hints is not a real gap. Brand Resolver returns officialDomain + aliases; host hints are synthesized downstream at searchDiscovery.js:373-378. No code expects or reads a dedicated host_hints input to the brand resolver.
INFO - resolveBrandDomain() receives the full config object even though only the LLM adapter meaningfully uses it.
INFO - Brand Resolver has no dedicated persisted input artifact; the contract exists in memory only.

content._doc: Brand Resolver output gap analysis. Compares the correction-note target for Brand Resolver against the actual output returned by resolveBrandDomain() and consumed in searchDiscovery(). Updated 2026-03-16.

downstream_consumers.search_profile_hints: searchDiscovery.js:373-378 and queryBuilder.js:943-950 reuse officialDomain + aliases as brandResolutionHints for later Search Profile planning.
downstream_consumers.manufacturer_auto_promote: searchDiscovery.js:223-258 uses officialDomain and supportDomain for source-host auto-promotion when config.manufacturerAutoPromote is enabled. Aliases are NOT promoted.
downstream_consumers.telemetry: searchDiscovery.js:202-219 logs brand_resolved with status, skip_reason, official_domain, aliases, support_domain, and confidence.

officialDomain.actual: MATCH - brandResolver.js:19 and 36 returns officialDomain from cache or normalized LLM output.

supportDomain.actual: MATCH - brandResolver.js:21 and 38 returns supportDomain from cache or normalized LLM output.

aliases.spec: Alias array for known brand-domain variants.
aliases.actual: MATCH - brandResolver.js:17 and 37 returns aliases from cache or normalized LLM output.
aliases._note: Aliases are text-only labels. They are NOT promoted as manufacturer hosts by manufacturerPromoter.js. Only officialDomain and supportDomain are promoted into the source registry.

host_hints.spec: Dedicated host_hints output for downstream search planning.
host_hints.actual: CLOSED — resolveBrandDomain() never returns a host_hints field. Hints are synthesized downstream from officialDomain + aliases at searchDiscovery.js:373-378. No consumer reads brandResolution.host_hints directly. This is by design, not a gap.

confidence.spec: Not required by the correction note.
confidence.actual: EXTRA - brandResolver.js:22 and 52 returns cached confidence or 0.8 after a successful LLM resolution.

reasoning.spec: Not required by the correction note.
reasoning.actual: EXTRA - brandResolver.js:24 and 39-52 returns an LLM-provided reasoning array; cached hits return reasoning: [].

no_direct_query_rows.spec: Brand Resolver should not emit direct final search rows.
no_direct_query_rows.actual: MATCH - resolveBrandDomain() returns only brand-domain metadata. It emits no queries, query_rows, or final search candidates.

failure_shape.spec: Safe empty output when Brand Resolver cannot or should not resolve.
failure_shape.actual: MATCH - brandResolver.js:10, 12, 27, and 54 returns { officialDomain: '', aliases: [], supportDomain: '', confidence: 0, reasoning: [] } on no brand, no callLlmFn, or exception.

artifact_persistence.spec: A standalone Brand Resolver artifact is optional.
artifact_persistence.actual: INFO - there is no dedicated persisted brand_resolver.json artifact today. The output is kept in memory, logged in searchDiscovery.js:166-180, and then folded into downstream Search Profile planning.

gaps.status: CLOSED — host_hints is not a real gap. Hints are synthesized downstream from officialDomain + aliases at searchDiscovery.js:373-378. No code reads brandResolution.host_hints.
EXTRA - useful telemetry field, but outside the correction-note contract.
EXTRA - useful telemetry/debug field, but outside the correction-note contract.
INFO - resolver output is not written as its own artifact today.

content._doc: Search Planner (buildSearchPlan) input contract. Documents the exact inputs consumed by searchPlanBuilder.js:269-276. This is the Schema 4 path — the NeedSet-driven single-call planner. Updated 2026-03-16.

call_boundary.function: buildSearchPlan({ searchPlanningContext, config, logger, llmContext, queryHashFn })

searchPlanningContext._doc: Schema 3 (SearchPlanningContext). Produced by buildSearchPlanningContext(). This is the primary input — everything the planner needs comes through here.

run.category: string — sent to LLM payload as identity.category
run.brand: string — sent to LLM payload as identity.manufacturer fallback
run.model: string — sent to LLM payload as identity.model fallback
run.base_model: string — not directly consumed by planner (passthrough to Schema 4)
run.aliases: string[] — not directly consumed by planner (passthrough to Schema 4)
run.round: number — sent to LLM payload; used for LLM usageContext

identity._note: Passthrough from Schema 2. Used as LLM identity.manufacturer/model fallback and emitted in Schema 4 panel.
identity.manufacturer: string|null — sent to LLM payload as identity.manufacturer
identity.model: string|null — sent to LLM payload as identity.model

needset.missing_critical_fields: string[] — CONSUMED: sent to LLM payload (searchPlanBuilder.js:312)
needset.unresolved_fields: string[] — NOT consumed by planner (per-group keys used instead)
needset.existing_queries: string[] — CONSUMED: sent to LLM payload (line 343) AND used for post-LLM dedup (lines 395-397)

planner_limits.phase2LlmEnabled: boolean — gates LLM call via hasLlmRouteApiKey (line 283)
planner_limits.discoveryMaxQueries: number — sent to LLM payload limits AND used for global cap (line 391)
planner_limits.maxUrlsPerProduct: number — sent to LLM payload limits (line 315)
planner_limits.llmModelPlan: string — resolved model for LLM call

group_catalog._tag: dropped (metadata already inlined into focus_groups at Schema 3 boundary)
group_catalog._note: buildSearchPlan does not read group_catalog — all catalog metadata (label, desc, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class) is already on each focus_group entry.

focus_groups._tag: core input — the planner's primary working data

_consumed_fields.key: string — sent to LLM payload; used for grouping queries in Schema 4 bundles
_consumed_fields.phase: string — 'now'|'next'|'hold'. GATE: only now/next groups are active (line 292)
_consumed_fields.label: string — passthrough to Schema 4 bundle
_consumed_fields.source_target: string — sent to LLM payload (line 338); passthrough to bundle
_consumed_fields.search_intent: string — sent to LLM payload (line 339); passthrough to bundle
_consumed_fields.host_class: string — sent to LLM payload (line 337); passthrough to bundle
_consumed_fields.field_keys: string[] — used for bundle fields[] derivation (line 200)
_consumed_fields.satisfied_field_keys: string[] — used for bundle fields[].state derivation + delta computation
_consumed_fields.search_exhausted_field_keys: string[] — not sent to LLM (phase=hold already filters these groups)
_consumed_fields.query_terms_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload CAPPED TO 5 (line 328)
_consumed_fields.domain_hints_union: string[] — FILTERED (dead domains removed, line 295) then sent to LLM CAPPED TO 5 (line 329)
_consumed_fields.preferred_content_types_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload CAPPED TO 5 (line 332)
_consumed_fields.existing_queries_union: string[] — FILTERED (dead hashes removed, lines 296-299) then sent to LLM CAPPED TO 5 (line 330)
_consumed_fields.domains_tried_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload CAPPED TO 5 (line 333)
_consumed_fields.host_classes_tried_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload UNCAPPED (line 334)
_consumed_fields.evidence_classes_tried_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload UNCAPPED (line 335)
_consumed_fields.aliases_union: string[] — sent to LLM payload CAPPED TO 5 (line 331)

field_priority_map._note: Map of field_key → required_level. Used in assembleSchema4 (line 189) to derive bundle fields[].bucket via requiredLevelToBucket().

learning.dead_query_hashes: string[] — hashes removed from existing_queries_union before LLM call (line 289-299)
learning.dead_domains: string[] — domains removed from domain_hints_union before LLM call (line 288-295)

previous_round_fields._note: Array<{ field_key, state }>. Used in computeDeltas() (line 91-121) to compute panel.deltas.

_consumed_keys.llmModelPlan: string — resolved model for the LLM call (line 347)

config._note: Full config object is forwarded to callLlmWithRouting(). Only the above keys are explicitly consumed by buildSearchPlan logic.

llmContext.recordUsage: function — async callback for usage row recording

queryHashFn._default: defaultQueryHash() — stableHash of lowercased, trimmed, space-normalized query text
queryHashFn._note: Used for dedup across existing queries and LLM-generated queries. Same algorithm as frontierDb.js (inlined to avoid coupling).

content._doc: Search Planner LLM call contract. Documents the exact prompt, payload shape, response schema, and post-processing rules for the Schema 4 NeedSet planner LLM call. Source: searchPlanBuilder.js:24-78 (schema+prompt), 305-374 (call), 383-434 (post-processing). Updated 2026-03-16.

call_site.gate: hasLlmRouteApiKey(config, { role: 'plan' }) — returns disabled result when false

system_prompt.role: search planner for hardware specification data collection
rules: Generate 1-3 queries per active group, each targeting different search strategies
Vary query families: manufacturer_html, manual_pdf, support_docs, review_lookup, benchmark_lookup, fallback_web, targeted_single
Prefer manufacturer docs, instrumented lab reviews, and trusted spec databases
Avoid junk domains, login pages, forums, and irrelevant topics
Do NOT repeat queries from existing_queries — generate DIFFERENT patterns
Use domains_tried_union to AVOID domains already visited — target DIFFERENT domains
Use host_classes_tried_union to diversify: if 'manufacturer' tried, try 'review' or 'database'
Use evidence_classes_tried_union to vary content types: if 'html' tried, target 'pdf' or 'json-ld'
When no_value_attempts is high (3+), radically change search strategy
weak_field_keys need CORROBORATION queries (authoritative sources to confirm)
conflict_field_keys need RESOLUTION queries (manufacturer/official sources to settle)
Use aliases_union to generate variant-aware queries
Each query must have 'family' (strategy type) and 'q' (search query text)
Optionally include 'target_fields', 'preferred_domains', and 'exact_match_required'
Return JSON with a 'groups' array where each group has 'key' and 'queries' array

focus_groups._note: Only groups with phase='now' or phase='next' (hold groups filtered out). Dead domains and dead query hashes removed before inclusion.

_per_group_shape.domain_hints_union: string[] — dead domains removed, CAPPED TO 5
_per_group_shape.existing_queries_union: string[] — dead hashes removed, CAPPED TO 5

response_schema._encoding: JSON Schema passed via callLlmWithRouting({ jsonSchema })

key._note: must match a focus_group key from the payload

reason_active._note: why this group is being searched

family._note: manufacturer_html | manual_pdf | support_docs | review_lookup | benchmark_lookup | fallback_web | targeted_single

target_fields._note: optional field keys this query targets

duplicates_suppressed._note: LLM self-reported dupe count (informational only — real dedup is post-LLM)

targeted_exceptions._note: exceptional queries added outside normal rules

response_normalization._note: Some models (e.g. Gemini) return a single group object { key, queries } instead of wrapping in { groups: [...] }. Both shapes are accepted.
response_normalization.array_groups: if llmResult.groups is an array, use directly
response_normalization.single_group: if llmResult.key exists and llmResult.queries is an array, wrap as [llmResult]

dedup._note: Queries are deduped by hash against (1) existing_queries from Schema 3 and (2) previously emitted queries in this call

capping.per_group_cap: 3 queries max per group (PER_GROUP_CAP constant, line 80)
capping.cap_overflow: queries exceeding either cap are counted as dupesDropped

query_assembly._note: Each accepted query is enriched with metadata before inclusion in Schema 4
query_assembly.q: string — trimmed query text from LLM
query_assembly.query_hash: string — deterministic hash for cross-round dedup
query_assembly.exact_match_required: boolean — from LLM or false fallback

error_handling.llm_call_failure: catch block (line 376-379) returns makeErrorResult(ctx, error) — mode='error', plannerComplete=false, error message captured
error_handling.no_api_key: hasLlmRouteApiKey gate (line 283) returns makeDisabledResult(ctx) — mode='disabled', plannerComplete=true, zero queries
error_handling.empty_llm_response: normalized to groups=[] — returns Schema 4 with zero queries but full panel/bundle metadata

content._doc: Search Planner (buildSearchPlan) output contract. Documents the exact Schema 4 (NeedSetPlannerOutput) shape returned by searchPlanBuilder.js:219-266 (assembleSchema4). This is what runDiscoverySeedPlan receives. Updated 2026-03-17.

_output_modes.llm: Normal path — LLM called, queries generated, full Schema 4 emitted
_output_modes.disabled: No API key — zero queries, plannerComplete=true, full panel/bundle metadata still emitted
_output_modes.error: LLM call failed — zero queries, plannerComplete=false, error message captured, full panel/bundle metadata still emitted

planner._tag: recomputed — describes what the planner did

planner_complete.source: true on success/disabled, false on error

duplicates_suppressed.source: count of queries dropped by hash dedup + cap overflow

search_plan_handoff._tag: the queries that feed into search execution

group_key.source: LLM group.key (must match Schema 3 focus_group key)

query_hashes._source: flat array of all queries[].query_hash values

panel._tag: GUI display payload — consumed by PrefetchNeedSetPanel and the embedded schema4 views in PrefetchSearchProfilePanel. Planner-call telemetry is rendered separately in PrefetchSearchPlannerPanel.

bundles._type: array — one per Schema 3 focus_group (all groups, including hold)

query_family_mix.source: LLM response per-group — null when LLM disabled/error or group not in LLM response

reason_active.source: LLM response per-group — null when LLM disabled/error or group not in LLM response

queries.source: projected from search_plan_handoff queries matching this group — array of { q, family }

_per_item.state: string — satisfied|weak|conflict|missing — derived from focus_group field key sets
_per_item.bucket: string — core|secondary|expected|optional — derived from field_priority_map via requiredLevelToBucket()

profile_influence.manufacturer_html: number — count of queries with this family

deltas._source: computeDeltas(ctx) — compares previous_round_fields vs current focus_group field states
deltas._note: On round 0 with no previous_round_fields, ALL fields are emitted as { from: 'none', to: <current_state> }

learning_writeback._tag: recomputed — summary for learning store persistence
learning_writeback._consumed_by: runDiscoverySeedPlan.js attaches to handoff as _learning; searchDiscovery.js threads into searchProfilePlanned.schema4_learning

domains_targeted.source: distinct preferred_domains from all queries, sorted

content._doc: Schema 4 search_plan_handoff shape — input to the handoff adapter. Produced by buildSearchPlan() in searchPlanBuilder.js, then enriched by runDiscoverySeedPlan.js with planner/learning/panel metadata. Updated 2026-03-16.

queries.query_hash: string — deterministic hash for dedup across rounds
queries.family: string — query family: manufacturer_html | manual_pdf | review_lookup | benchmark_lookup | support_docs | fallback_web | targeted_single
queries.group_key: string — field group this query targets (e.g. sensor_performance, physical)
target_fields: string — field keys this query is expected to resolve
preferred_domains: string — domains to prefer for this query (used as domain_hint)
queries.exact_match_required: boolean — whether results must exact-match the product identity

query_hashes: string — flat array of all query_hash values for dedup

_metadata_enrichment._doc: runDiscoverySeedPlan.js attaches these underscore-prefixed fields to the handoff object after Schema 4 completes. They are NOT part of the buildSearchPlan() output — they are added at the orchestration layer so searchDiscovery.js can thread them into searchProfilePlanned.

_planner.model: string — LLM model used for planning
_planner.planner_complete: boolean — whether the planner finished successfully
_planner.planner_confidence: number — overall plan confidence 0-1 from LLM response
_planner.targeted_exceptions: number — exceptional queries added outside normal rules
_planner.error: string | null — error message if mode is 'error'

query_hashes_generated: string — hashes of all generated queries
queries_generated: string — raw query text of all generated queries
families_used: string — query families represented in the plan
domains_targeted: string — preferred domains across all queries
groups_activated: string — field groups that received queries

_panel.bundles: Array<{ key, label, desc, priority, phase, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class, query_family_mix, reason_active, queries[], fields[] }>
_panel.deltas: Array<{ field, from, to }> — round-over-round changes

content._doc: Cumulative pipeline output at the end of Stage 3: search plan → search execution → post-execution processing. Documents the full searchProfileFinal shape (the persisted search_profile artifact) and the discoverCandidateSources() return value. Updated 2026-03-16.

_pipeline_data_accumulated.schema_1: NeedSetStartInput — per-field provenance, identity context, field rules, round context
_pipeline_data_accumulated.schema_2: NeedSetOutput — field states, summary, blockers, planner_seed, history feedback
_pipeline_data_accumulated.schema_3: SearchPlanningContext — focus_groups with inlined catalog metadata, anti-garbage unions, search exhaustion, aliases, learning, previous_round_fields
_pipeline_data_accumulated.schema_4: NeedSetPlannerOutput — LLM-generated queries, planner confidence, panel bundles with fields[]/deltas, learning writeback
_pipeline_data_accumulated.search_execution: raw SERP results, search attempts, search journal
_pipeline_data_accumulated.post_execution: SERP dedup, domain classification, admission filtering, deterministic + LLM triage reranking, serp_explorer, discovered URLs

searchProfileFinal._doc: The persisted search_profile artifact. Built as { ...searchProfilePlanned, ...execution_additions }. Written to storage via writeSearchProfileArtifacts(). Available on discoverCandidateSources() return as result.search_profile.

_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.base_model: string — job.baseModel || '' — variant base model for shared-spec queries
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.aliases: string[] — job.aliases || [] — known product name variants for diverse queries
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.selected_queries: string[] — query strings that passed identity guard
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.query_rows: Array<QueryRow> — per-query metadata (overridden by enriched rows in final)
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.effective_host_plan: object|null — v2 host plan (null for Schema 4 path, populated for old path)
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.brand_resolution: { officialDomain, supportDomain, aliases, confidence, reasoning }|null — from brand resolver
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.schema4_planner: { mode, planner_confidence, duplicates_suppressed, targeted_exceptions }|null — Schema 4 planner metadata
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.schema4_learning: { query_hashes_generated, queries_generated, families_used, domains_targeted, groups_activated, duplicates_suppressed }|null — Schema 4 learning writeback
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.schema4_panel: { round, round_mode, identity, summary, blockers, bundles, profile_influence, deltas }|null — Schema 4 full panel payload
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.key: string — S3 input key for search profile artifact
_from_searchProfilePlanned_schema4_path.latest_key: string — S3 latest key (overwritten each run)

_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path._note: Old path spreads searchProfileBase (from buildSearchProfile) then overrides. Has all the same keys as Schema 4 path plus:
_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path.llm_queries: Array — raw LLM-generated query objects (old path only, forward investment for review GUI)
_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path.source: NOT SET — old path doesn't set a 'source' field
_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path.schema4_planner: null — always null on old path
_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path.schema4_learning: null — always null on old path
_from_searchProfilePlanned_old_path.schema4_panel: null — always null on old path

_from_execution_additions.generated_at: string — overridden: ISO timestamp of execution completion
_from_execution_additions.query_rows: Array<EnrichedQueryRow> — overridden: original rows enriched with { result_count, attempts, providers, frontier_cache }
_from_execution_additions.query_stats: Array<QueryAttemptStat> — per-query execution stats: { query, result_count, attempts, providers, frontier_cache }
_from_execution_additions.discovered_count: number — total URLs that passed all filters
_from_execution_additions.candidate_count: number — URLs on non-approved domains (candidates for fetch)
_from_execution_additions.llm_query_planning: boolean — whether LLM query planning was enabled
_from_execution_additions.llm_query_model: string — LLM model used for query planning
_from_execution_additions.llm_serp_triage: boolean — whether LLM SERP triage was enabled and applied
_from_execution_additions.llm_serp_triage_model: string — LLM model used for SERP triage
_from_execution_additions.serp_explorer: object — full SERP explorer payload (see below)

serp_explorer._doc: SERP explorer — per-query breakdown of all candidate URLs with triage decisions.
serp_explorer.llm_triage_applied: boolean — was LLM triage actually applied (skipped when deterministic quality sufficient)
serp_explorer.llm_triage_model: string — LLM model used for triage
serp_explorer.candidates_checked: number — total unique URLs seen across all queries
serp_explorer.urls_triaged: number — URLs that entered triage (deterministic or LLM)
serp_explorer.urls_selected: number — URLs that made the final discovered list
serp_explorer.dedupe_input: number — raw SERP results before dedup
serp_explorer.dedupe_output: number — SERP results after cross-provider dedup

queries.hint_source: string — where this query originated (v2.host_plan, schema4_search_plan, etc.)
target_fields: string — field keys this query targets
queries.result_count: number — raw results returned by search provider
providers: string — search providers that executed this query
queries.candidate_count: number — URLs from this query that entered triage
queries.selected_count: number — URLs from this query in final discovered list

candidates.title: string (truncated to 220 chars)
candidates.doc_kind: string — guessed document type (spec_sheet, product_page, review, pdf, etc.)
candidates.triage_score: number — composite score from deterministic reranker or LLM triage
reason_codes: string — up to 8 reason codes (approved_domain, tier_1, brand_match, model_match, domain_hint_match, etc.)
providers: string — search providers that returned this URL

discoverCandidateSources_return._doc: The full return value from discoverCandidateSources(). This is what runDiscoverySeedPlan receives back.
discoverCandidateSources_return.discoveryKey: string — S3 key for discovery artifact
discoverCandidateSources_return.candidatesKey: string — S3 key for candidates artifact
discoverCandidateSources_return.approvedUrls: string[] — URLs on approved domains (fed to planner.enqueue)
discoverCandidateSources_return.candidateUrls: string[] — URLs on non-approved domains (fed to planner.seedCandidates)
discoverCandidateSources_return.llm_queries: Array — raw LLM-generated query objects (forward investment)
discoverCandidateSources_return.search_profile: searchProfileFinal — the full cumulative profile (see above)
discoverCandidateSources_return.query_concurrency: number — concurrent query execution limit used
discoverCandidateSources_return.internal_satisfied: boolean — whether internal sources satisfied the search need
discoverCandidateSources_return.external_search_reason: string — reason external search was triggered
discoverCandidateSources_return.search_attempts: Array<SearchAttempt> — per-query execution log with result counts
discoverCandidateSources_return.serp_explorer: object — same serp_explorer as on searchProfileFinal
discoverCandidateSources_return.seed_search_plan_output: object|null — full Schema 4 output (needset_planner_output.v2) attached by runDiscoverySeedPlan() after discovery. Contains panel, search_plan_handoff, planner, learning_writeback. Used by finalization derivation to enrich needSet without re-calling the LLM. Only present when config.enableSchema4SearchPlan is true and schema4 computation succeeded.

adapter_conversion._doc: convertHandoffToExecutionPlan() in searchPlanHandoffAdapter.js. Converts Schema 4 handoff queries into the shape executeSearchQueries() expects. This is an internal intermediate — NOT the cumulative output.
queries: string — flat array of query strings extracted from handoff
adapter_conversion.selectedQueryRowMap: Map<lowercase_query, RowObject> — lookup by lowercase query

queryRows.domain_hint: string — first preferred_domain or empty string

_notes.deduplication: Duplicate q values are removed (first wins). Falsy q values are skipped.
_notes.identity_guard: After conversion, enforceIdentityQueryGuard() filters out off-brand/off-model queries before execution.
_notes.interface_parity: executeSearchQueries() receives queries and selectedQueryRowMap in exactly the same shape as the old 7-layer path.

content._doc: Query Journey input contract. Documents the exact inputs consumed at each sub-phase of Stage 4: Schema 4 handoff → adapter → guard → execution → classification → rerank/triage. Updated 2026-03-16.

queries.note: flat array of q strings, deduped by lowercase

queryRows.note: array of { query, source:'schema4_planner', target_fields, domain_hint (first preferred_domain), doc_hint:'', hint_source:'schema4_search_plan', family, group_key, query_hash }

transforms.preferred_domains → domain_hint: first preferred_domain becomes domain_hint; rest dropped
transforms.dedup: duplicate q values (case-insensitive) are removed; first wins
transforms.falsy_filter: queries with empty/null q are skipped

input.variantGuardTerms: string[] — additional variant aliases to reject

guardContext.modelTokens: string[] — compact model tokens (excluding brand tokens + generic)
guardContext.allowedModelTokens: string[] — normalized model tokens + variants with trim/decomposition

validation_rules: All brand tokens must appear in query (case-insensitive)
If model has digit groups (e.g. 'g502'), ALL digit groups must appear
If no digit groups but model tokens exist (>=4 chars), at least one model token must appear
Foreign model-like tokens (not in allowedModelTokens) → reason 'foreign_model_token:<token>'
Queries failing ANY check are rejected; passing queries are returned unchanged

input.queries: string[] — accepted query strings from guard
input.resultsPerQuery: number — target results per query from provider

rawResults.note: { url, title, snippet, provider, query, rank, ... } from search providers

searchJournal.note: { ts, query, provider, action, reason, result_count, duration_ms }

internalSatisfied.note: true if internal results met required field target

externalSearchReason.note: why external search was triggered or skipped

execution_flow: 2. Internal-first phase (if discoveryInternalFirst) — frontier cache + corpus search
3. Internet search phase — SearXNG with concurrency, per-query frontier check
4. Plan-only fallback — if no provider, generate URLs from manufacturer domain
5. Record to query index (NDJSON) and frontier DB

multi_model_hint.note: true if comparison/listing page pattern detected

input.config: object — weight map, LLM model settings, timeouts
input.serpResults: array — classified URL candidates from phase 4
input.topK: number — default 16, max results to rerank
input.domainSafetyResults: Map<string, { safe: boolean }> — optional domain safety check

score_breakdown.note: 14-key breakdown: base, frontier, identity, variant, multi_model, tier, host_health, operator_risk, field_affinity, diversity, needset_coverage, brand_presence, model_presence, spec_manual

keep.note: boolean — LLM decision or always true

content._doc: Query Journey LLM call contract. Stage 4 has exactly ONE optional LLM call: the SERP reranker/triage in rerankSerpResults(). All other phases are deterministic. Updated 2026-03-16.

serp_reranker_llm_call._call_site: inner LLM call within rerank phase

conditions: quality threshold check (deterministic scores indicate LLM value)
gate.fallback: deterministic scoring only — all results get keep=true, rerank_reason='deterministic'

text: You rerank search results for evidence-first hardware spec extraction.
Keep URLs with strong identity match and likely field relevance.
Drop low-value, duplicate, or dead-pattern URLs.

_per_item.title: string — truncated to 240 chars
_per_item.score_det: number — deterministic score rounded to 4 decimal places

response_schema._encoding: JSON Schema passed via callLlmWithRouting({ jsonSchema })

url._note: must match a URL from the payload

keep._note: true to keep, false to drop

response_processing.url_matching: LLM selected_urls matched by url string to input results
response_processing.unmatched_urls: URLs not in LLM response get keep=true (default keep) or keep=false if LLM explicitly dropped all
response_processing.score_fallback: If LLM provides no score, falls back to score_det

error_handling.llm_call_failure: Falls back to deterministic scores, rerank_reason='deterministic_fallback'
error_handling.empty_selections: If LLM returns 0 selected_urls → deterministic fallback
error_handling.explicit_all_drop: If LLM explicitly drops ALL URLs → returns { explicitAllDrop: true, fallbackReason: 'llm_explicit_all_drop' }

deterministic_scoring._note: Always computed regardless of LLM path. Used as baseline or fallback.

content._doc: Query Journey output contract. Documents the cumulative output of Stage 4 at each sub-phase boundary, ending with the discovered URL candidates that feed into Stage 5 (fetch/parse). Updated 2026-03-16.

queries.note: deduped flat array of query strings

rows._type: array — subset of input rows that passed validation
rows._note: rows are unchanged — only the set is reduced

rawResults._tag: enriched — new data from search providers

query.source: the query that produced this result

phase_4_classification_output._function: classifyUrlCandidate() — applied to each result after dedup

admission_hard_rejects._note: These filters run BEFORE reranking. Hard-rejected URLs never reach the reranker.
admission_hard_rejects.url_cooldown: URL is in frontier cooldown window
admission_hard_rejects.manufacturer_brand_mismatch: tier 1 source but brand does not match identity lock
admission_hard_rejects.low_relevance: none identity match AND no field affinity

admission_soft_exclusions._note: These filters run after classification but before final discovery list.

keep.note: LLM keep/drop decision or always true

_final_discovery_list._note: After reranking, results are filtered by keep=true, capped by serpTriageMaxUrls, and split into approved/candidate URLs.
_final_discovery_list.approvedUrls: string[] — URLs on approved domains → fed to planner.enqueue()
_final_discovery_list.candidateUrls: string[] — URLs on non-approved domains → fed to planner.seedCandidates()

searchProfileFinal._note: The searchProfilePlanned object is enriched with execution results and written to storage.
searchProfileFinal.query_rows: overridden with enriched rows including result_count, attempts, providers
searchProfileFinal.discovered_count: total URLs that passed all filters
searchProfileFinal.llm_query_planning: boolean — whether LLM planning was enabled
searchProfileFinal.llm_serp_triage: boolean — whether LLM triage was applied
searchProfileFinal.serp_explorer: full SERP explorer payload (see stage 3 handoff-output.json)

cumulative_artifacts_written.discovery_payload: written to S3 with candidate URLs and approved URL lists
cumulative_artifacts_written.query_index: NDJSON per-query execution log for observability

content._doc: Final query payload that hits SearXNG via runSearchProviders(). Identical regardless of Schema 4 or old path origin.
queries: string — the actual search strings sent to SearXNG

target_fields: string — fields this query aims to resolve (used for post-triage field attribution)
per_query_metadata.domain_hint: string — preferred domain (used for site: prefix by some providers)
per_query_metadata.doc_hint: string — expected document type (spec_sheet, review, manual)
per_query_metadata.hint_source: string — origin of the hint: schema4_search_plan | targeted | host_plan | base_template

execution_config.searchProvider: string — dual | searxng | bing | google | duckduckgo

execution_phases: 3. Plan-only fallback (if no provider available) via buildPlanOnlyResults()

output.internalSatisfied: boolean — internal corpus met required field targets
output.externalSearchReason: string | null — why external search was run/skipped

content._doc: Search Planner LLM call contract. Documents the exact prompt, payload shape, response schema, and post-processing rules for the Schema 4 NeedSet planner LLM call. Source: searchPlanBuilder.js:24-78 (schema+prompt), 305-374 (call), 383-434 (post-processing). Updated 2026-03-16.

call_site.gate: hasLlmRouteApiKey(config, { role: 'plan' }) — returns disabled result when false

system_prompt.role: search planner for hardware specification data collection
rules: Generate 1-3 queries per active group, each targeting different search strategies
Vary query families: manufacturer_html, manual_pdf, support_docs, review_lookup, benchmark_lookup, fallback_web, targeted_single
Prefer manufacturer docs, instrumented lab reviews, and trusted spec databases
Avoid junk domains, login pages, forums, and irrelevant topics
Do NOT repeat queries from existing_queries — generate DIFFERENT patterns
Use domains_tried_union to AVOID domains already visited — target DIFFERENT domains
Use host_classes_tried_union to diversify: if 'manufacturer' tried, try 'review' or 'database'
Use evidence_classes_tried_union to vary content types: if 'html' tried, target 'pdf' or 'json-ld'
When no_value_attempts is high (3+), radically change search strategy
weak_field_keys need CORROBORATION queries (authoritative sources to confirm)
conflict_field_keys need RESOLUTION queries (manufacturer/official sources to settle)
Use aliases_union to generate variant-aware queries
Each query must have 'family' (strategy type) and 'q' (search query text)
Optionally include 'target_fields', 'preferred_domains', and 'exact_match_required'
Return JSON with a 'groups' array where each group has 'key' and 'queries' array

focus_groups._note: Only groups with phase='now' or phase='next' (hold groups filtered out). Dead domains and dead query hashes removed before inclusion.

_per_group_shape.domain_hints_union: string[] — dead domains removed, CAPPED TO 5
_per_group_shape.existing_queries_union: string[] — dead hashes removed, CAPPED TO 5

response_schema._encoding: JSON Schema passed via callLlmWithRouting({ jsonSchema })

key._note: must match a focus_group key from the payload

reason_active._note: why this group is being searched

family._note: manufacturer_html | manual_pdf | support_docs | review_lookup | benchmark_lookup | fallback_web | targeted_single

target_fields._note: optional field keys this query targets

duplicates_suppressed._note: LLM self-reported dupe count (informational only — real dedup is post-LLM)

targeted_exceptions._note: exceptional queries added outside normal rules

response_normalization._note: Some models (e.g. Gemini) return a single group object { key, queries } instead of wrapping in { groups: [...] }. Both shapes are accepted.
response_normalization.array_groups: if llmResult.groups is an array, use directly
response_normalization.single_group: if llmResult.key exists and llmResult.queries is an array, wrap as [llmResult]

dedup._note: Queries are deduped by hash against (1) existing_queries from Schema 3 and (2) previously emitted queries in this call

capping.per_group_cap: 3 queries max per group (PER_GROUP_CAP constant, line 80)
capping.cap_overflow: queries exceeding either cap are counted as dupesDropped

query_assembly._note: Each accepted query is enriched with metadata before inclusion in Schema 4
query_assembly.q: string — trimmed query text from LLM
query_assembly.query_hash: string — deterministic hash for cross-round dedup
query_assembly.exact_match_required: boolean — from LLM or false fallback

error_handling.llm_call_failure: catch block (line 376-379) returns makeErrorResult(ctx, error) — mode='error', plannerComplete=false, error message captured
error_handling.no_api_key: hasLlmRouteApiKey gate (line 283) returns makeDisabledResult(ctx) — mode='disabled', plannerComplete=true, zero queries
error_handling.empty_llm_response: normalized to groups=[] — returns Schema 4 with zero queries but full panel/bundle metadata

content._doc: Query Journey LLM call contract. Stage 4 has exactly ONE optional LLM call: the SERP reranker/triage in rerankSerpResults(). All other phases are deterministic. Updated 2026-03-16.

serp_reranker_llm_call._call_site: inner LLM call within rerank phase

conditions: quality threshold check (deterministic scores indicate LLM value)
gate.fallback: deterministic scoring only — all results get keep=true, rerank_reason='deterministic'

text: You rerank search results for evidence-first hardware spec extraction.
Keep URLs with strong identity match and likely field relevance.
Drop low-value, duplicate, or dead-pattern URLs.

_per_item.title: string — truncated to 240 chars
_per_item.score_det: number — deterministic score rounded to 4 decimal places

response_schema._encoding: JSON Schema passed via callLlmWithRouting({ jsonSchema })

url._note: must match a URL from the payload

keep._note: true to keep, false to drop

response_processing.url_matching: LLM selected_urls matched by url string to input results
response_processing.unmatched_urls: URLs not in LLM response get keep=true (default keep) or keep=false if LLM explicitly dropped all
response_processing.score_fallback: If LLM provides no score, falls back to score_det

error_handling.llm_call_failure: Falls back to deterministic scores, rerank_reason='deterministic_fallback'
error_handling.empty_selections: If LLM returns 0 selected_urls → deterministic fallback
error_handling.explicit_all_drop: If LLM explicitly drops ALL URLs → returns { explicitAllDrop: true, fallbackReason: 'llm_explicit_all_drop' }

deterministic_scoring._note: Always computed regardless of LLM path. Used as baseline or fallback.
